# Effect Desktop

> Effect Desktop is a desktop application framework with three process boundaries — a Rust host that owns the native shell, a Bun runtime that owns application services, and a renderer (React, Solid, Vue, or your own) that owns UI. Every privileged operation crosses a typed Effect service. Permissions are deny-by-default. Resources are scoped. Failures are tagged.

The framework is **pre-v1**. Workspace packages are private at version `0.0.0` and not yet published to npm; consumers develop against this repository directly. APIs are stable in shape but not in version.

The documentation follows the [Diátaxis](https://diataxis.fr/) framework: tutorials (learning), how-to guides (tasks), reference (lookup), explanation (understanding).

## Start here

- [Install](docs/start/install.md): Toolchain, requirements, verify the workspace
- [Build your first app](docs/start/first-app.md): Five-minute zero-to-running walkthrough
- [Where to go next](docs/start/next-steps.md): Pick a tutorial, recipe, reference page, or essay

## Tutorials

- [Build a notes app](docs/tutorials/01-build-a-notes-app.md): Settings, RPC, React, end-to-end
- [Add a second window](docs/tutorials/02-add-a-second-window.md): Multi-window state and restoration
- [Stream from the runtime](docs/tutorials/03-stream-from-the-runtime.md): Long-running jobs with cancellation
- [Package, sign, and ship](docs/tutorials/04-package-and-sign.md): Build pipeline through Apple notarization

## Reference — top-level

- [Desktop API](docs/reference/desktop-api.md): `make`, `manifest`, `app`, `window`, `Rpc.surface`
- [Desktop.Rpc surfaces](docs/reference/rpc-surface.md): `surface`, `supportedGroup`, generated clients
- [Configuration](docs/reference/config.md): `defineDesktopConfig`, schema, production checks
- [CLI commands](docs/reference/cli.md): every subcommand and report
- [Errors catalog](docs/reference/errors.md): every typed failure
- [Devtools](docs/reference/devtools.md): inspector shell and panels
- [Platform browser](docs/reference/platform-browser.md): IndexedDB, SQLite WASM, PGlite

## Explanation

- [Architecture overview](docs/explanation/architecture.md): three process roles, one envelope
- [The boundary rule](docs/explanation/boundary-rule.md): why renderers never get raw native authority
- [Permissions model](docs/explanation/permissions-model.md): deny-by-default, decision order, audit
- [Layer-first design](docs/explanation/layer-first-design.md): how public services compose
- [RPC surface vs. mapped surface](docs/explanation/rpc-surface-vs-mapped.md): when to use each
- [Resource lifecycle](docs/explanation/resource-lifecycle.md): scopes, ownership, cleanup
- [Audit and redaction](docs/explanation/audit-and-redaction.md): every privileged call observable
- [Testability](docs/explanation/testability.md): substitutable layers
- [Effect-first philosophy](docs/explanation/effect-first-philosophy.md): why thin wrappers are debt

## Optional

- [`engineering/SPEC.md`](engineering/SPEC.md): full v1.0.0 build specification (source of truth for status, milestones, verification matrix)
- [`engineering/architecture/layer-first-contract.md`](engineering/architecture/layer-first-contract.md): the contract every public capability follows
- [`apps/inspector/`](apps/inspector): the only first-party app, useful as a reference scaffold
